metadata.title: letter from Royal Thai Legation to President A.H. Upham, 1942-06-02
metadata.creator: anonymous
metadata.description: A letter from the Royal Thai Legation's Student Department to President A.H. Upham of Miami University expressing appreciation for hospitality and admiration for the university's work.
metadata.transcript:
ROYAL THAI LEGATION
STUDENT'S DEPARTMENT

2300 KALORAMA ROAD
WASHINGTON, D.C.

June 2, 1942

President A. H. Upham
Miami University
Oxford, Ohio

Dear President Upham:

Just a line, upon my return to the
Legation, to thank you for the many courtesies which were
shown to me while I was at the University, and to tell you
again how proud I am of the wonderful work which you are
doing there. There is a charm and a dignity about the place
which I had never so thoroughly felt before. If only I could
now be looking backward to the pleasure of having been the
agency through whom you secured yesterday's Commencement
speaker, my cup would indeed be running over. I shall still
hope in that direction!

Kindest regards to Mrs. Upham and yourself.

Sincerely yours,
[HANDWRITTEN: Murray [UNCLEAR]]
